crime.value: Death of Perry Hobbs by drowning in creek, possibly after being drugged
crime.quote: Whether someone drugged him before his drowing him in the creek is only known by the murderer.

victim.value: Perry Hobbs, a violent abusive man who beat his partners and terrorized the village
victim.quote: Perry Hobbs moved to Springfield about six months ago and stole peace from the village.

closed_world.value: Springfield village - a small community where everyone knows each other's business
closed_world.quote: Everybody in the village knew that he

culprit_and_motive.value: Implied to be Pastor Joshua Black, motivated by hatred of Hobbs' violence and abuse
culprit_and_motive.quote: But no one could even dare to assume to know how I hated Hobbs and wished him to go to hell.

resolution.value: Case officially closed as accident by Sheriff Collister, true resolution left ambiguous
resolution.quote: The death of Perry Hobbs was an accident, a perfect storm.

investigator.value: Pastor Joshua Black as narrator/suspect and Sheriff Collister as official investigator

suspect_architecture.value: Multiple suspects with distinct motives: Pastor Black (narrator under suspicion, hatred of victim), Mayor Murphy (illegitimate daughter connection), Sheriff Collister (wanted to teach victim a lesson, questioned alibi), plus village context of widespread relief at victim's death
suspect_architecture.quote: Janet Tanner´s child was his illegitimate daughter. He confessed that to me.

red_herring.value: Drowning staged as accident with potential drug involvement - sheriff dismisses narcotic evidence and closes case as accidental death despite murder implications
red_herring.quote: I will play it down with the narcotic, just like the doping data at the Tour de France

clue_fairness.value: Reader given same information as investigator about suspects' motives, confessional secrets, and the narrator's own hatred of the victim
clue_fairness.quote: No one could read my thoughts.

social_world.value: Religious hierarchy and village power structure - Pastor holds confessional secrets, Mayor has political position to protect, Sheriff controls investigation, domestic violence tolerated by community
social_world.quote: God sees everything, preached the unmarried Joshua Black with all passion to his church

alibi.value: Sheriff Collister's whereabouts questioned for midnight timeframe, though specific alibi details not provided in excerpt
alibi.quote: And where exactly was Mr. Collister around midnight?

media_and_audience.value: Village perception of victim as tyrant whose death brought freedom - public relief at Perry Hobbs' death shapes narrative around the crime
media_and_audience.quote: Many in the village are slightly happy, that Hobbs is dead

investigator_wound.value: Pastor Black as narrator-investigator struggles with his own hatred of the victim and moral complicity - his Christian beliefs conflict with his personal desires for the victim's demise
investigator_wound.quote: no one could even dare to assume to know how I hated Hobbs and wished him to go to hell

_meta.title: ebook elvira frankenheim the evil within
